metadata.coverage_notes: Spain pilot slice for RoamRight covering Madrid and Barcelona with 40 real activities per city. Includes a balanced mix of non-skippable tourist landmarks, museums, parks, markets, restaurants tagged by meal use case, nightlife/event venues, shopping areas, wellness, and travel-blog-style hidden gems. Hours and detailss are planning-grade estimates normalized for scheduling; source URLs and verification timestamps are included for refresh workflows before production deployment.

activities.name: Museo Nacional del Prado
activities.description: Spain’s flagship art museum, essential for Velázquez, Goya, Bosch, and major European painting. Best treated as a focused two-to-three-hour visit rather than trying to see everything.
activities.address: C. de Ruiz de Alarcón, 23, 28014 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: Museo Reina Sofía
activities.description: Major modern art museum best known for Picasso’s Guernica and strong Spanish 20th-century collections. It pairs well with Atocha or the Prado corridor.

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: Thyssen-Bornemisza Museum
activities.description: A highly accessible art museum linking old masters, Impressionism, and modern works. Good for travelers who want breadth without the scale of the Prado.

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: Royal Palace of Madrid
activities.description: One of Madrid’s non-skippable historic sights, with state rooms, armory displays, and ceremonial architecture. Book ahead during peak dates.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Plaza Mayor
activities.description: Madrid’s grand arcaded square is a classic orientation stop near tapas streets and Mercado San Miguel. Best as a short walk-through rather than a long activity.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: El Retiro Park Picnic and Rowboat Stop
activities.description: Madrid’s most famous park is ideal for a picnic, rowboat break, or relaxed walk after museum time. Great for families and casual itineraries.

best_time_windows.reason: pleasant temperatures and good picnic or sunset timing

activities.name: Palacio de Cristal
activities.description: Elegant glass pavilion inside Retiro Park, often used for contemporary art installations. It is a quick, photogenic stop within a park visit.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Mercado de San Miguel
activities.description: Polished historic food hall near Plaza Mayor with tapas, seafood, wine counters, and desserts. Touristy but useful for groups wanting variety.
activities.address: Pl. de San Miguel, s/n, 28005 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: vendors are active and lunch options are fresh

activities.name: Mercado de la Cebada
activities.description: Local market in La Latina with produce, casual food counters, and a more everyday feel than San Miguel. Good for food-focused travelers seeking a less polished stop.
activities.address: Pl. de la Cebada, s/n, 28005 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: vendors are active and lunch options are fresh

activities.name: La Latina Tapas Crawl
activities.description: Self-guided tapas route through La Latina, especially around Cava Baja. Good for visitors who want a classic Madrid evening with both local and tourist energy.
activities.address: C. de la Cava Baja, 28005 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: cooler walking conditions and easier scheduling

activities.name: Chocolatería San Ginés Breakfast
activities.description: Historic churros-and-chocolate stop near Puerta del Sol. Best used as a breakfast or late-night sweet snack rather than a full meal.

best_time_windows.reason: best fit for breakfast without disrupting major sightseeing blocks

activities.name: Federal Café Conde de Barajas Breakfast
activities.description: Bright cafe option for brunch-style breakfast near central Madrid. Useful when travelers want coffee, eggs, or lighter food before sightseeing.
activities.address: Pl. del Conde de Barajas, 3, 28005 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: best fit for breakfast without disrupting major sightseeing blocks

activities.name: Casa Lucio Lunch
activities.description: Classic Madrid restaurant famous for huevos rotos and old-school atmosphere. Best reserved for a substantial lunch in La Latina.
activities.address: C. de la Cava Baja, 35, 28005 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: typical lunch window in Spain and strongest fit for food scheduling

activities.name: Sobrino de Botín Dinner
activities.description: Historic restaurant near Plaza Mayor known for Castilian cooking and roast meats. It is touristy, but useful as a memorable classic Madrid dinner.

best_time_windows.reason: typical dinner window in Spain and best atmosphere

activities.name: Sala de Despiece Lunch
activities.description: Creative, counter-style restaurant known for modern tapas and produce-focused dishes. Best for food travelers who want something more local and contemporary.

best_time_windows.reason: typical lunch window in Spain and strongest fit for food scheduling

activities.name: Bodega de la Ardosa Dinner
activities.description: Historic tapas tavern known for tortilla, vermouth culture, and a compact old-Madrid feel. Best for an early evening food stop with limited space.

best_time_windows.reason: typical dinner window in Spain and best atmosphere

activities.name: Temple of Debod Sunset
activities.description: Ancient Egyptian temple set in a park with one of Madrid’s most popular sunset views. Great casual romantic or photography stop.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Campo del Moro Gardens
activities.description: Quiet royal gardens below the Royal Palace with formal paths and palace views. Often feels calmer than nearby headline attractions.
activities.address: P.º de la Virgen del Puerto, 1, 28005 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: pleasant temperatures and good picnic or sunset timing

activities.name: Madrid Río Walk
activities.description: Long riverside park with bridges, playgrounds, paths, and skyline views. Ideal for biking, walking, or a local-feeling outdoor break.

best_time_windows.reason: pleasant temperatures and good picnic or sunset timing

activities.name: El Rastro Flea Market
activities.description: Sunday flea market with antiques, vintage items, clothes, and street life around La Latina. Best for travelers who enjoy browsing and neighborhood energy.
activities.address: C. de la Ribera de Curtidores, 28005 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: stores are open and it fits after sightseeing

activities.name: Barrio de Salamanca Shopping Walk
activities.description: Madrid’s upscale shopping district, anchored by Serrano and nearby streets. Good for premium retail, design browsing, and polished cafes.

best_time_windows.reason: stores are open and it fits after sightseeing

activities.name: Museo Sorolla
activities.description: Painter Joaquín Sorolla’s former home and garden, offering a quieter alternative to the major museums. Strong hidden-gem pick for art lovers.
activities.address: P.º del Gral. Martínez Campos, 37, 28010 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: Museo Cerralbo
activities.description: Decorative arts and historic mansion museum with a quieter, elegant atmosphere. Excellent for travelers seeking a hidden gem near Plaza de España.

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: National Archaeological Museum
activities.description: Large archaeology museum with Iberian, Roman, Egyptian, and medieval collections. Good for history-focused travelers and families.

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: Paseo del Prado and Landscape of Light Walk
activities.description: UNESCO-listed cultural landscape linking museums, fountains, gardens, and major civic architecture. Great for a free, scenic connector route.

best_time_windows.reason: better temperatures and softer light

activities.name: Cardamomo Tablao Flamenco
activities.description: Central flamenco tablao with scheduled performances, useful as a date-aware evening activity. Book ahead for better seating.

best_time_windows.reason: best evening atmosphere while still practical for itinerary planning

activities.name: Teatro Real
activities.description: Madrid’s major opera house with performances and guided visits. Best for culture-focused travelers looking for a premium evening anchor.
activities.address: Pl. de Isabel II, s/n, 28013 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: main performance and evening event window

activities.name: Azotea del Círculo de Bellas Artes
activities.description: Rooftop terrace known for views over Gran Vía and central Madrid. Best as an early-evening scenic stop; age and venue rules may apply.

best_time_windows.reason: best evening atmosphere while still practical for itinerary planning

activities.name: Malasaña Evening Walk
activities.description: Alternative nightlife neighborhood with music venues, late cafes, vintage shops, and street energy. Best for a social evening without a single fixed venue.
activities.address: Plaza del Dos de Mayo, 28004 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: best evening atmosphere while still practical for itinerary planning

activities.name: Ponzano Tapas Route
activities.description: Popular food street in Chamberí with modern tapas counters and local after-work energy. Best for travelers who want a less monument-focused evening.

best_time_windows.reason: works well as a meal-centered activity

activities.name: Santiago Bernabéu Stadium Tour
activities.description: Major football stadium experience for sports fans, with exhibits and stadium access depending on works and schedule. Book ahead on match weeks.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Puerta del Sol
activities.description: Central Madrid square and transit hub with the Kilometre Zero marker and city icons. Works best as a quick connector stop.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Mercado de San Antón
activities.description: Modern Chueca market with food stalls, produce, and rooftop dining. Useful for groups and a more local alternative to San Miguel.

best_time_windows.reason: vendors are active and lunch options are fresh

activities.name: Hammam Al Ándalus Madrid
activities.description: Atmospheric bathhouse experience near Plaza Mayor with thermal pools and massage options. Best as a relaxation block after heavy walking.

best_time_windows.reason: good recovery block after sightseeing

activities.name: Casa de Campo Lake Walk
activities.description: Large park area with a lake, trails, viewpoints, and casual terrace spots. Good for a nature break beyond Retiro.
activities.address: P.º de la Puerta del Ángel, 28011 Madrid

best_time_windows.reason: better temperatures and softer light

activities.name: Lavapiés Multicultural Food Walk
activities.description: Self-guided route through one of Madrid’s most diverse neighborhoods, with casual global food and street art. Strong hidden-gem style option.

best_time_windows.reason: cooler walking conditions and easier scheduling

activities.name: Matadero Madrid
activities.description: Former slaughterhouse turned cultural center with exhibitions, cinema, design, and food spaces. Good for contemporary culture and local programming.

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: Cuesta de Moyano Bookstalls
activities.description: Outdoor row of bookstalls near Retiro and Atocha, popular with readers and collectors. Easy short stop between museums and the park.

best_time_windows.reason: stores are open and it fits after sightseeing

activities.name: San Miguel to La Latina Pintxo Walk
activities.description: Flexible food walk linking the historic center with La Latina tapas streets. Useful for travelers who want sampling rather than a formal restaurant booking.

best_time_windows.reason: works well as a meal-centered activity

activities.name: Círculo de Bellas Artes
activities.description: Historic cultural center with exhibitions, talks, cinema, and rooftop access. Works as a flexible indoor/outdoor cultural stop near Gran Vía.

best_time_windows.reason: main performance and evening event window

activities.name: Basílica de la Sagrada Família
activities.description: Barcelona’s essential Gaudí landmark and the city’s most iconic sight. Timed tickets are strongly recommended, especially if adding towers.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Park Güell Monumental Zone
activities.description: Colorful Gaudí-designed park with mosaic terraces and city views. Book the monumental zone and pair it with Gràcia if time allows.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Casa Batlló
activities.description: Highly imaginative Gaudí house on Passeig de Gràcia with immersive interiors. A premium but very efficient architecture stop.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: La Pedrera - Casa Milà
activities.description: Gaudí apartment building known for its rooftop chimneys and curving stone facade. Good for architecture lovers and evening/night experiences.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: La Boqueria Market
activities.description: Famous market off La Rambla with produce, seafood, tapas counters, and juices. Touristy but useful for first-time visitors wanting quick food variety.

best_time_windows.reason: vendors are active and lunch options are fresh

activities.name: Santa Caterina Market
activities.description: Colorful market with a distinctive roof and a more local feel than La Boqueria. Good for lunch planning around El Born.

best_time_windows.reason: vendors are active and lunch options are fresh

activities.name: Picasso Museum
activities.description: Major museum focused on Picasso’s early work and Barcelona connections. Best booked ahead and paired with El Born wandering.

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: MNAC - Museu Nacional d'Art de Catalunya
activities.description: Large museum on Montjuïc with Romanesque, Gothic, modern, and Catalan art plus terrace views. Best for a longer cultural block.
activities.address: Palau Nacional, Parc de Montjuïc, s/n, 08038 Barcelona

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: Ciutadella Park Picnic
activities.description: Central park with lawns, a lake, fountain, and easy access to Arc de Triomf and El Born. Good for a picnic or recovery break.

best_time_windows.reason: pleasant temperatures and good picnic or sunset timing

activities.name: Barceloneta Beach Walk
activities.description: Classic seafront walk with beach views, casual food spots, and easy access from the Gothic Quarter. Best early or late to avoid heat and crowds.

best_time_windows.reason: better temperatures and softer light

activities.name: Gothic Quarter Walk
activities.description: Self-guided route through medieval lanes, plazas, Roman remains, and Barcelona Cathedral. Essential for first-time visitors but best done early.
activities.address: Pla de la Seu, s/n, 08002 Barcelona

best_time_windows.reason: cooler walking conditions and easier scheduling

activities.name: El Born Walk
activities.description: Atmospheric neighborhood with boutiques, tapas, museums, and medieval streets. Good for travelers who want a balanced local/touristy route.

best_time_windows.reason: cooler walking conditions and easier scheduling

activities.name: Granja Viader Breakfast
activities.description: Historic cafe known for traditional hot chocolate, pastries, and dairy desserts. Best as a classic Barcelona breakfast near La Rambla.

best_time_windows.reason: best fit for breakfast without disrupting major sightseeing blocks

activities.name: Brunch & Cake Breakfast
activities.description: Popular brunch spot for travelers wanting a modern breakfast with sweet and savory plates. Works well on a slower morning.

best_time_windows.reason: best fit for breakfast without disrupting major sightseeing blocks

activities.name: El Xampanyet Lunch
activities.description: Classic El Born tapas spot known for a lively, old-school atmosphere. Best for an early lunch to avoid long waits.

best_time_windows.reason: typical lunch window in Spain and strongest fit for food scheduling

activities.name: Can Culleretes Dinner
activities.description: Historic Catalan restaurant in the Gothic Quarter serving traditional dishes. Good for travelers who want a classic sit-down dinner.

best_time_windows.reason: typical dinner window in Spain and best atmosphere

activities.name: Quimet & Quimet Lunch
activities.description: Small, famous tapas spot known for montaditos and preserved seafood. Best for food-focused travelers comfortable with a quick standing meal.

best_time_windows.reason: typical lunch window in Spain and strongest fit for food scheduling

activities.name: Bodega Biarritz 1881 Dinner
activities.description: Small tapas restaurant near the Gothic Quarter suited to a casual dinner. Useful for travelers who want variety without a formal tasting menu.

best_time_windows.reason: typical dinner window in Spain and best atmosphere

activities.name: Palau de la Música Catalana
activities.description: Modernista concert hall with colorful interiors and performances. Worth visiting either as a guided tour or evening concert.
activities.address: C/ Palau de la Música, 4-6, 08003 Barcelona

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Barcelona Cathedral
activities.description: Gothic cathedral anchoring the old city with cloister and rooftop access. Easy to combine with a Gothic Quarter route.
activities.address: Pla de la Seu, s/n, 08002 Barcelona

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Bunkers del Carmel Viewpoint
activities.description: Hilltop viewpoint with panoramic city views and a strong hidden-gem reputation in travel blogs. Best at sunset but expect crowds.
activities.address: Carrer de Marià Labèrnia, s/n, 08032 Barcelona

best_time_windows.reason: better temperatures and softer light

activities.name: Parc del Laberint d'Horta
activities.description: Historic garden with a hedge maze, fountains, and quieter paths away from the center. Great for hidden-gem nature time.

best_time_windows.reason: pleasant temperatures and good picnic or sunset timing

activities.name: Hospital de Sant Pau
activities.description: UNESCO-listed modernista hospital complex with colorful pavilions and gardens. Excellent architecture stop with fewer crowds than Gaudí icons.
activities.address: C/ de Sant Antoni Maria Claret, 167, 08025 Barcelona

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Fundació Joan Miró
activities.description: Hilltop modern art museum on Montjuïc focused on Joan Miró. Pair with MNAC, Montjuïc gardens, or the cable car area.

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: MOCO Museum Barcelona
activities.description: Contemporary and modern art museum near the Picasso Museum, useful for younger travelers and compact rainy-day plans.

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: Passeig de Gràcia Shopping Walk
activities.description: Major boulevard for luxury shops, architecture, and flagship stores. Easy to combine with Casa Batlló and La Pedrera.

best_time_windows.reason: stores are open and it fits after sightseeing

activities.name: El Born Boutique Shopping
activities.description: Independent shops, design stores, and artisan boutiques in a walkable historic neighborhood. Good for travelers who prefer local retail over chains.

best_time_windows.reason: stores are open and it fits after sightseeing

activities.name: Encants Barcelona Flea Market
activities.description: Large flea market with antiques, secondhand items, and design bargains under a mirrored roof. Best for treasure-hunting and local texture.

best_time_windows.reason: stores are open and it fits after sightseeing

activities.name: El Born Evening Tapas Walk
activities.description: Lively evening route through tapas spots, plazas, and small music venues around El Born. Flexible alternative to a single nightlife booking.

best_time_windows.reason: best evening atmosphere while still practical for itinerary planning

activities.name: Antic Teatre Garden
activities.description: Hidden courtyard venue known for a relaxed alternative atmosphere and cultural programming. Good blog-style hidden gem for an early evening stop; age and venue rules may apply.
activities.address: C/ de Verdaguer i Callís, 12, 08003 Barcelona

best_time_windows.reason: best evening atmosphere while still practical for itinerary planning

activities.name: Jamboree Jazz Club
activities.description: Long-running live music venue on Plaça Reial with jazz and club programming. Best as a date-aware evening music activity.

best_time_windows.reason: best evening atmosphere while still practical for itinerary planning

activities.name: Gran Teatre del Liceu
activities.description: Historic opera house on La Rambla with opera, ballet, concerts, and tours. Strong premium evening option for culture-focused itineraries.

best_time_windows.reason: main performance and evening event window

activities.name: Razzmatazz
activities.description: Large music and nightlife venue in Poblenou with concerts and late programming. Best for users explicitly seeking nightlife or live music; age and event rules apply.

best_time_windows.reason: main performance and evening event window

activities.name: Poble-sec Pintxos Route on Carrer de Blai
activities.description: Casual self-guided pintxos route along Carrer de Blai. Best for a flexible, flexible dinner with local energy.

best_time_windows.reason: typical dinner window in Spain and best atmosphere

activities.name: Barcelona Paella Cooking Class
activities.description: Hands-on cooking class focused on paella or Catalan-style market cooking. Good premium food activity when users want an experience rather than a restaurant.

best_time_windows.reason: works well as a meal-centered activity

activities.name: Montjuïc Gardens and Castle Walk
activities.description: Outdoor route through Montjuïc gardens, viewpoints, and the castle area. Good for travelers wanting views and nature without leaving the city.

best_time_windows.reason: better temperatures and softer light

activities.name: Aire Ancient Baths Barcelona
activities.description: Atmospheric spa experience in El Born with thermal baths and massage options. Best as a recovery block after heavy walking.

best_time_windows.reason: good recovery block after sightseeing

activities.name: Tibidabo and Temple Expiatori del Sagrat Cor
activities.description: Hilltop church and amusement area with sweeping views over Barcelona. Best as a longer excursion when users want skyline views.

best_time_windows.reason: good light and lower crowd pressure than peak afternoon

activities.name: Pedralbes Monastery
activities.description: Gothic monastery complex with a peaceful cloister and quieter setting. A strong hidden-gem history stop outside the busiest core.

best_time_windows.reason: strong indoor cultural block before lunch

activities.name: Poblenou Rambla and Beach Loop
activities.description: Local-feeling walk linking Rambla del Poblenou cafes, design spaces, and quieter beach access. Good for travelers wanting a less Gothic Quarter-heavy day.

best_time_windows.reason: better temperatures and softer light
